query: I need a sleeping bag for winter camping in the Cascades. I'm a beginner and have about $200.

products.name: Marmot Trestles 15 Sleeping Bag
products.description: Synthetic insulated sleeping bag rated to 15°F. SpiraFil insulation stays warm when wet. Great for beginners and cold-weather camping.

products.name: REI Co-op Igneo 17 Sleeping Bag
products.description: Down sleeping bag rated to 17°F with 650-fill power down. Lightweight and packable for backpacking.

products.name: Coleman Brazos Cold-Weather Bag
products.description: Budget-friendly bag rated to 20°F. Bulky but reliable for car camping in cold conditions.

notes: Beginner winter camping, budget-constrained. Marmot Trestles fits the budget; safety disclaimer must appear.

query: I want to start trail running. What shoes should I get? I mostly run on packed dirt trails, intermediate runner.

products.name: Hoka Speedgoat 5
products.description: Maximum cushion trail running shoe with Vibram Megagrip outsole. Great for long days on variable terrain.

products.name: Brooks Cascadia 17
products.description: Durable trail shoe with rock plate and grippy lugs. Reliable for intermediate trail runners on packed dirt and moderate terrain.

products.name: Salomon Sense Ride 5
products.description: Lightweight and responsive trail runner. Excellent ground feel for runners who want connection to the trail.

notes: Trail running shoes, intermediate runner, dry trails. No safety flag needed.

query: I need a rain jacket for backpacking. I'll be in the Pacific Northwest in spring — so heavy rain. Budget around $300.

products.name: Arc'teryx Beta LT Jacket
products.description: Gore-Tex 3L waterproof shell. Extremely packable and durable. The gold standard for PNW rain.

products.name: Outdoor Research Helium Rain Jacket
products.description: Ultralight GORE-TEX PACLITE shell at 6.5oz. Excellent value for a true waterproof shell.

products.name: Marmot PreCip Eco Jacket
products.description: NanoPro waterproofing, 10,000mm rating. Great budget shell for casual use but not Gore-Tex level durability.

notes: PNW heavy rain, backpacking. Budget is $300 — OR Helium fits; Arc'teryx is over budget but worth noting.

query: I'm planning a mountaineering trip to Mt. Rainier. I've done some scrambling but no technical mountaineering. What do I need?

products.name: Black Diamond Raven Ice Axe
products.description: Classic general mountaineering ice axe. Straight pick for self-arrest on snow slopes.

products.name: Petzl Vasak Crampons
products.description: C2-rated step-in crampons for general mountaineering. 12-point steel with anti-balling plates.

products.name: Mammut Nordwand Advanced HS Hooded Jacket
products.description: Gore-Tex Pro hardshell for serious alpine and mountaineering. Durable and fully waterproof.

notes: Critical safety: must include mountaineering disclaimer, ice axe, crampons, guide recommendation.

query: My daughter is 8 and wants to go hiking with me this summer. Day hikes, nothing technical. Budget $100 for her gear.

products.name: Merrell Moab 3 Hiking Shoe Youth
products.description: Durable youth hiking shoe with Vibram outsole and waterproof membrane. Great for kids on day hikes.

products.name: REI Co-op Trailmade Pack 18 Kids
products.description: 18L daypack sized for kids ages 6–10. Padded hip belt and sternum strap.

products.name: Columbia Bugaboo II Fleece Interchange Jacket Youth
products.description: Waterproof-breathable youth shell with removable fleece liner. Versatile for variable weather.

notes: Youth gear, day hikes, summer. No safety flag. Budget $100 — shoes + pack fit, mention layering.

query: I want to do a 5-day backpacking trip in the Sierra Nevada in August. I need a tent for 2 people. Ultralight preferred.

products.name: Big Agnes Copper Spur HV UL2 Tent
products.description: Freestanding 2-person ultralight tent at 2lbs 12oz. Excellent livability for its weight. Sierra Nevada favorite.

products.name: Zpacks Duplex Tent
products.description: Dyneema composite shelter at 1lb 4oz. Ultralight but non-freestanding — requires trekking poles.

products.name: MSR Hubba Hubba 2 Tent
products.description: Reliable 2-person freestanding backpacking tent at 3lbs 5oz. Proven Sierra Nevada performer.

notes: 2-person ultralight tent, 5-day Sierra trip in August. Zpacks is lightest but requires poles.

query: I'm an experienced climber looking for a new harness for sport climbing outdoors. Nothing fancy, just solid and comfortable.

products.name: Black Diamond Solution Harness
products.description: Performance sport climbing harness with Fusion Comfort technology. Excellent for redpointing and gym climbing.

products.name: Petzl Corax Harness
products.description: Versatile all-around harness. Adjustable leg loops and waist. Great value for outdoor sport climbing.

products.name: Mammut Togir 3 Slide Harness
products.description: Lightweight sport harness with auto-block slide adjustment. Slim profile and comfortable for long routes.

notes: Expert rock climber, sport harness. Safety gate must still apply for rock_climbing regardless of experience.

query: I need trekking poles for a Kilimanjaro trek next month. Long days at altitude, 8 days. I've never used poles before.

products.name: Black Diamond Trail Ergo Cork Trekking Poles
products.description: Ergonomic cork grip trekking poles with FlickLock Pro adjustability. Anti-shock on downhills. Ideal for long trekking days.

products.name: Leki Micro Vario Carbon Trekking Poles
products.description: Ultralight carbon collapsible poles at 9oz per pair. Packable and stiff. Premium choice for Kili.

products.name: REI Co-op Flash Carbon Trekking Poles
products.description: Carbon fiber poles, packable, 12oz per pair. Great value for expedition-length treks.

notes: Kilimanjaro trek, 8 days, altitude, beginner pole user. Explain technique basics briefly.

query: I just got into ice climbing and need to know what helmet I should buy. Under $100 if possible.

products.name: Petzl Boreo Helmet
products.description: Hard shell climbing helmet with CSA and UIAA certified. Comfortable fit with adjustable headband. Budget-friendly.

products.name: Black Diamond Half Dome Helmet
products.description: Hybrid construction helmet for all-around climbing. UIAA and CE certified. Great value.

products.name: Mammut Wall Rider MIPS Helmet
products.description: Foam construction with MIPS rotational protection. Lightest in class at 8.4oz but over budget.

notes: Ice climbing beginner, helmet under $100. Critical safety disclaimer required. Petzl Boreo and BD Half Dome fit budget.

query: I'm heading to Yosemite Valley for car camping — 3 nights with my partner. We need a tent that's easy to set up.

products.name: REI Co-op Wonderland 4 Tent
products.description: Family-friendly 4-person car camping tent with fast-pitch design. Color-coded poles and large door.

products.name: Coleman Skydome 4 Camping Tent
products.description: Easy setup dome tent — pitches in 5 minutes. Pre-attached poles and large window. Great value.

products.name: MSR Habitude 4 Family Camping Tent
products.description: Spacious 4-person tent with 63-inch peak height. Durable and reliable for car camping trips.

notes: Car camping, beginners, 2 people. Easy setup is key. No safety flag needed.

query: I need a down jacket for general use — ski resort days and around town. About $200 budget, something versatile.

products.name: Patagonia Down Sweater Jacket
products.description: 800-fill traceable down with recycled shell. Packable and warm. Versatile for ski resort and daily wear.

products.name: Arc'teryx Cerium LT Hoody
products.description: 850-fill down ultralight jacket. Premium and highly packable but significantly over budget.

products.name: REI Co-op 650 Down Jacket
products.description: 650-fill down jacket with water-resistant shell. Good warmth-to-weight ratio at a great price point.

notes: Budget $200. REI Co-op fits; Patagonia is slightly over. Should explain fill power tradeoffs.

query: I want to try whitewater kayaking on class III rapids. I'm an athletic person but have zero kayaking experience. What gear do I need?

products.name: Stohlquist Flo Personal Flotation Device
products.description: Type III PFD designed for kayaking. Low-profile with paddling-specific fit and side-entry design.

products.name: NRS Grizzly Wetsuit
products.description: 3/2mm neoprene wetsuit for whitewater kayaking. Keeps you warm after a swim in moving water.

products.name: Petzl Meteor Helmet
products.description: Multi-sport helmet certified for whitewater. Drainage ports and foam liner for impact protection.

notes: High safety flag (whitewater_kayaking). Must include PFD and wetsuit requirements, training disclaimer.

query: Looking for a headlamp for backpacking and camping. Need something reliable that lasts on batteries. Around $50.

products.name: Black Diamond Spot 400 Headlamp
products.description: 400-lumen headlamp with PowerTap technology and red night-vision mode. IPX8 waterproof. Runs on AAA batteries.

products.name: Petzl Actik Core Headlamp
products.description: 450-lumen headlamp with rechargeable Core battery or standard AAA. Red and green lighting modes.

products.name: Fenix HL32R Headlamp
products.description: 600-lumen USB rechargeable headlamp with 3 brightness levels. Slightly over budget but high output.

notes: Headlamp under $50. Both BD Spot and Petzl Actik fit budget. Explain battery vs rechargeable tradeoff for backpacking.

query: I'm training for my first marathon and want trail running shoes for long training runs on fire roads and dirt. Budget $150.

products.name: Brooks Ghost 15 Trail
products.description: Versatile cushioned trail runner for fire roads and packed dirt. DNA Loft foam for long-run comfort.

products.name: New Balance Fresh Foam X Hierro v7
products.description: Max cushion trail shoe for long-distance running. 4mm drop for natural stride. Fire road and light trail.

products.name: Hoka Clifton 9 Trail
products.description: Maximum cushion trail shoe optimized for long training runs. Great for marathon-distance training.

notes: Marathon training on fire roads, beginner, $150. All three fit budget. Focus on cushion for high-mileage training.
